info.name: Azure DevOps Wiki API
info.description:
REST API for creating and managing wikis and wiki pages in Azure DevOps projects. Every project wiki is backed by a Git repository, enabling version-controlled documentation. Supports creating, reading, updating, and deleting wikis and pages programmatically with Markdown content.


Contact Support:
 Name: Microsoft Azure DevOps

item.name: Azure DevOps Get a wiki page

query.description: (Required) Azure DevOps REST API version. Use 7.1 for the latest stable version.
Wiki page path (e.g., /MyPage or /Folder/SubPage)
Recursion level for subpages
Version descriptor (branch or tag) to read the page from
Whether to include the page content in the response

request.description: Returns the content and metadata for a wiki page at a given path. The path parameter is required to specify which page to retrieve. Returns the Markdown content of the page and optional metadata such as git commit information.

response.name: Wiki page returned successfully

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

query.description: (Required) Azure DevOps REST API version. Use 7.1 for the latest stable version.
Wiki page path (e.g., /MyPage or /Folder/SubPage)
Recursion level for subpages
Version descriptor (branch or tag) to read the page from
Whether to include the page content in the response

description.content: Entity tag for the current version of the page (version hash)

response.name: Unauthorized - missing or invalid authentication credentials

item.name: Azure DevOps Create or update a wiki page

query.description: (Required) Azure DevOps REST API version. Use 7.1 for the latest stable version.
(Required) Path of the wiki page to create or update
Comment for the commit creating this page update
Branch to create or update the page on

request.description: Creates a new wiki page or updates an existing page at the specified path. The request body must contain the Markdown content for the page. To update an existing page, include the current page version in the If-Match header to prevent overwriting concurrent changes.

response.name: Wiki page updated successfully

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

query.description: (Required) Azure DevOps REST API version. Use 7.1 for the latest stable version.
(Required) Path of the wiki page to create or update

item.name: Azure DevOps Delete a wiki page

query.description: (Required) Azure DevOps REST API version. Use 7.1 for the latest stable version.
(Required) Path of the wiki page to delete
Comment for the deletion commit
Branch to delete the page from

request.description: Deletes a wiki page at the specified path. This commits a deletion to the backing Git repository. Deleted pages cannot be recovered except through the backing repository's Git history.

response.name: Wiki page deleted successfully, returns the deleted page

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

request.description: Returns detailed information about a specific wiki, including its backing Git repository ID, mapped path (for code wikis), and current version. The wikiIdentifier can be either the wiki GUID or its name.

request.description: Deletes a wiki. For a project wiki, this deletes the wiki and the backing Git repository. For a code wiki, this removes the wiki but leaves the backing repository intact. This operation cannot be undone.

response.name: Wiki deleted successfully, returns the deleted wiki

request.description: Creates a new wiki for a project. You can create a project wiki (backed by a dedicated wiki repository) or a code wiki (backed by an existing Git repository and folder path). Only one project wiki can exist per project.
